title: 第 53 節
en: — the hawk of heaven, 805. Agnis, the five, in India and Persia, Agriology of the future, 231.
Ahans, the two bright ones, day and night, 558, 561.
Ahi, the serpent, slain by Indra, 758.
Ahti or Ahto, Finnish water deity, Ahto, see Ahti.
Aijeke vetschera, Finnish, the hammer of Ukko, from Zend vazra ?
Aikagryam, concentration of the mind and the senses, 224.
Aithra (clear sky), mother of Theseus, — the captive of the Dioskouroi, and the slave of Helena, 526. Akka, or Eukko, mother, Finnish goddess, 262. Akmon, 507.

Akria of Argos, the Dawn, 725. Aletis, a name of Fortuna, 778. Alexander, 52, 61, 62. Alkmene, representative of the morning? 524- — mother of Herakles, 520, 612, 613. 'AXXrjyopla, description of one thing under the image of another, 73. Allelotheism and Henotheism, 146.

Allelotheistic character of Vedic gods, 53iAltaic tribes, five classes of, 256. Amenti, Egyptian, 833. America, solar mythology in, 163. Amesha-spentas and Adityas, 552. Ammarik, the Gloaming (fem.), in Finnish mythology, 267. Amphion, the daily sun, parijrman, — married to Niobe, 643.
— and Zethos, the twin sons of Zeus and Antiope, 522, 578, 642. Amphitryon, husband of Adkmene, — and parigrnan, 614.
Amntas, immortals, their origin, 116, Anadyomene, or Aphrodite, 172, 731. Analogical School of Comparative Mythology, 117, 180, 234. Analogy, its influence on language, 31 8.
